question_text: A distance-time graph shows distance on the vertical axis and time on the horizontal axis. What does a steep line on this graph tell you about the object's motion?
correct_answer: A steep line means the object is moving quickly (or has a high speed).
explanation: On a distance-time graph, the steeper the line, the greater the distance covered in the same amount of time, which means the object is moving faster. A flat horizontal line would mean the object is stationary.
